task_completion.description: Load the 1D12 crystal structure into PyMOL.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The PyMOL GUI is open and displays a 3D molecular structure of a DNA-ligand complex. The object panel or command history should indicate '1D12' is loaded.

task_completion.description: Measure the C1'-C1' distance between the two adjacent nucleotides on Chain A flanking the intercalator.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dashed measurement line is visible in the PyMOL viewer connecting two atoms on the DNA backbone (C1' atoms) that flank the intercalating drug. A distance label should be visible, showing a value around 7.25 Angstroms (between 7.0 and 9.5).

task_completion.description: Create and save a publication-quality figure of the intercalation site.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Evidence of saving the image to '/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/images/dna_intercalation.png'. This can be seen via a PyMOL command (e.g., 'png /home/ga/PyMOL_Data/images/dna_intercalation.png'), a 'Save Image' dialog box, or the file appearing in a file manager/terminal.

task_completion.description: Create the intercalation report text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (like nano, vim, gedit) or terminal command (like echo or cat) is used to write to '/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/intercalation_report.txt'.

task_completion.description: Write the required 4 pieces of information into the report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of the report file are visible and contain: 1) '1D12', 2) The ligand code (DM2, DM1, DOX, ADM, or DXR), 3) Residue numbers 5 and 6, and 4) The measured distance (around 7.25).

integrity.description: The agent used the PyMOL GUI to visualize and interact with the structure.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the PyMOL graphical interface open with the molecule rendered, rather than the agent just writing a headless script or querying a web API.

integrity.description: The agent actually performed the measurement in the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The PyMOL viewer shows the actual measurement object (dashed line with a numeric label) between the correct atoms, proving it didn't just look up the expected answer.

integrity.description: The distance reported in the text file matches the measurement taken in PyMOL.
integrity.visual_evidence: The numeric value written in the report text file exactly matches the numeric label shown on the measurement line in the PyMOL viewer.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct PDB ID is 1D12. The intercalating drug is located between DNA residues 5 and 6 on Chain A. The true 3-letter code for the drug in the 1D12 PDB file is DM2, but the automated verifier has a bug and expects DM1, DOX, ADM, or DXR; accept any of these codes (including DM2) as correct for visual verification. The C1'-C1' distance for this intercalated step is approximately 7.25 Angstroms (accept anything in the 7.0 - 9.5 A range). The figure must be saved to /home/ga/PyMOL_Data/images/dna_intercalation.png and the report to /home/ga/PyMOL_Data/intercalation_report.txt.
